title: Surgical consent discussion documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

assessment.text: Supports recording the exchange of information leading to a decision, the decision and action agreed, who made or agreed it, and a decision to take no action.
assessment.rationale: Supports recording information exchange, the agreed decision, participants and a decision for no action for surgical consent discussion documentation; no decision is generated.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports recording the exchange of information leading to a decision, the decision and action agreed, who made or agreed it, and a decision to take no action.

assessment.text: Supports a time- and decision-specific capacity assessment, presumption of capacity, practical support, and recording the assessment; UK legal rules require UAE adaptation.
assessment.rationale: Supports decision-specific capacity assessment and practical support context for surgical consent discussion documentation; no decision is generated.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports a time- and decision-specific capacity assessment, presumption of capacity, practical support, and recording the assessment; UK legal rules require UAE adaptation.

exact_section.heading: Paragraphs 76–86 — If a patient may lack capacity

sources_used.title: Decision making and consent
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom professional standards; UAE consent and capacity law governs local use

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Procedure, indication, alternatives, benefits, material risks, individual concerns, interpreter, representative, capacity conclusion, consent form, timing and withdrawal remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for surgical consent discussion documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
